title: Runtime Journeys
description: Recorded user journeys walked against a sandboxed instance of the target application (skill 09). Stored at .work/semantic-loop/runtime/journeys.json. Optional artefact — absent when the runtime phase did not run (no approval, missing dependencies, or the target would not start); its absence is a recorded unknown, never a gate failure. Observed evidence confirms and adds, never vetoes (EV-6/RT-5). NORMALIZATION RULE (RT-8): this artefact and every trace file it references must be written pre-normalized — no wall-clock timestamps, no session identifiers (jsessionid path segments and cookie values stripped), no Set-Cookie or Date header values, no durations, no absolute filesystem paths outside the runtime directory. Two runs over the same repository state and database snapshot must produce byte-identical journeys.json and trace files after this normalization; screenshot files are exempt from byte-identity (rendering is not deterministic) and are compared by recorded hash integrity only.

produced_by.description: Work-relative path of the journey script that produced this artefact, e.g. 'runtime/scripts/walk-journeys.mjs'.

approval.description: RT-3: executing the target is a trust-boundary change and requires explicit user approval before anything is launched.

statement.description: Where/how the user granted approval for this run.

environment.description: RT-6: declared runtime dependencies, checked before launch. A missing dependency triggers the RT-4 degradation path instead of a failed run.

db_snapshot.description: RT-2: journeys run against a disposable copy of the runtime database; the committed source file stays byte-identical. Both identities are recorded so the verifier can prove it.

source_file.description: Repository-relative path of the committed database file.

source_sha256.description: sha256 of the committed file at launch time; the verifier recomputes it to prove the source tree was untouched.

copy_path.description: Work-relative path of the disposable copy the application actually used.

flow_hypothesis.description: What predicted this journey: a doc-claims claim id and/or the statically derived route chain it walks. Journeys are driven by hypotheses, not free exploration.

actor.description: The identity the journey ran as, e.g. 'operator1'.

corroborates.description: Semantic-graph node ids this journey corroborates (EntryPoint/Action/View). Corroboration confirms and adds; it never lowers or removes anything (EV-6).

url.description: Context-relative URL, normalized (no scheme/host/port, no jsessionid).

route.description: The framework route this URL maps to (matches a Route source node's path), e.g. '/login'.

form_fields.description: Names (never values) of form fields submitted in this step; matched against form-bean properties.

redirect_location.description: Normalized Location of a redirect response, when one occurred.

rendered_view.description: Webapp-relative template the rendered page was matched to, e.g. '/jsp/taskList.jsp'.

view_evidence.description: What in the rendered page matched the view (title, heading, form action).

db_diff.description: Observed table-level row-count changes on the disposable copy caused by this step; null when not measured (corroboration-only slice).
